logical_communication_definition: federated model downloads and uploads only

protocol_assumptions: All 50,000 standard training examples are partitioned among clients with no internal validation collection.
The final-round model is evaluated once on the official test collection, which the paper calls validation.
The released source monitors the official test collection during training; this project does not.
The project trains selected clients only, whereas the released program computes all client updates before selecting uploads.
Uniform aggregation follows released Fed.py and conflicts with the paper's example-count-weighted equation.

interpretations: Final-round selection, one official-test access after round 100, isolated random generators, and training selected clients only are project protocol choices.
paper: Table I reports the IID and alpha-0.5 non-IID SNN 10/2 rows after 100 rounds and three repetitions.
Five local epochs, batch size 32, SGD learning rate 0.1, momentum 0.95, and reductions after rounds 40, 60, and 80 are paper-reported settings.
released_source: Signed normalization and Poisson encoding, 20 timesteps, temporal-mean readout, Xavier gain-2 initialization, BNTT epsilon 0.0001, dropped local remainders, and uniform aggregation are source-derived.
Weight decay 0.0001 is inherited from the pinned source default and is not explicit in the paper.
